mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations during periods of rising INDPRO over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing domestic demand and weakening construction activity, jointly foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly change < 0 and HOUST monthly change < 0
failure_condition: Instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously do not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and forthcoming earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE has risen more than 0.2pp in 3 months during late-cycle conditions, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly real GDP growth signals contracting aggregate demand and falling corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with negative GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls increase by more than 150 thousand in a month, labor market strength reduces near-term recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while consumer prices are accelerating, stagflationary supply constraints squeeze corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3%
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising consumer price index erodes real corporate earnings and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL is rising.
